what_it_does: A token-driven CSS stylesheet that defines the Surface application's chrome, three authored layouts (desktop, portrait, landscape), and the visual styling for the wheel visualization, disclosure panels, controls, scrubber, gallery overlay, decisions inbox, tools panel, settings, and form engine.
observations: Uses only CSS custom properties (var()) for colors, spacing, radii, typography, and elevation; no literal hex colors
Implements three layout modes: default desktop grid (.stage), portrait (.layout--portrait), and landscape (.layout--landscape) with a rail
Contains extensive inline comments that read as design critiques and rationales for specific rules
Defines a fixed z-index layering system (scrubber 20, tools 25, sheet 30, settings/lenschip 41, notice 50, inbox 55, gallery 60, verify banner 9000)
Includes an animated breathing indicator, a pulsing live dot, and a reduced-motion media query
Provides a verify-mode banner that appears only for ?verify URLs, styled with caution pigment and pointer-events none
Styles a selection tether as a fixed SVG overlay with a dotted stroke linking a selected dot to its detail card
Caps the gallery overlay frame at 390px to enforce a phone-archetype modal width on desktop
Uses BEM-like class naming with block--modifier and block-element patterns
standards: The heavy use of design-critic comments to justify layout decisions (e.g., portrait wheel aspect-ratio, optics cluster wrapping, gallery scrim opacity)
The selection tether overlay with opacity driven by a rAF loop in Tether.tsx
The verify banner at z-index 9000 as a loud dry-run signal
The gallery face overlay as a single focused modal above all chrome
conventions: Token-only styling via var() references, presumably defined in paper.css
BEM-style class names with --modifier and -element suffixes
Component-scoped sections separated by comment headers
Responsive behavior through layout modifier classes rather than media queries, with a single desktop media query for disclosure panel width
Consistent modal idiom shared across inbox, tools, and gallery overlays
concerns: Some layout dimensions are hardcoded in px (e.g., 264px, 320px, 390px, 56px, 40px) despite the token-only mandate for colors/spacing/radii
z-index values are magic numbers without a documented token map in this file
The file content is truncated in the provided excerpt, so the full rule set and any closing media queries are not visible
A few rules use !important to suppress outline/box-shadow on SVG sectors
notes: This file is the visual contract for the Surface UI: it encodes not just styling but the design rationale behind layout, layering, and component behavior.
questions: Where are the CSS custom properties such as --safe-t, --ink-primary, --pig-born defined (paper.css)?
How are the .layout--portrait and .layout--landscape classes assigned at runtime?
What component mounts the .gallery-frame content (the DNA render host mentioned in comments)?
What is the full content after the truncation point, especially any remaining media queries or component styles?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose—Surface chrome plus the three authored layouts using token-only styling—matches the content, which is almost entirely layout and component styling driven by CSS variables.
apparent_themes: Calm, paper-like visual language with careful hierarchy
Embedded design rationale and critique as documentation
Fail-loud signals (verify banner, notices, strain pigment)
intention_signals: The file appears to be trying to implement a polished, multi-form-factor visualization surface where every visual decision is explicitly justified, components share a single modal idiom, and the UI remains calm and legible across desktop, portrait, and landscape orientations.
novelty: The extensive design-critic commentary embedded directly in CSS comments is unusual; it turns the stylesheet into a ledger of visual decisions. The selection tether, verify banner, and gallery-face phone-archetype modal are also distinctive.
contribution: A comprehensive, token-driven CSS vocabulary for the Surface application's layouts, overlays, and controls, including explicit design rationale and component interaction patterns that a fused system would retain for consistent chrome and responsive behavior.
summary_for_embedding: A token-only CSS stylesheet defining the Surface app's chrome, three responsive layouts, wheel visualization, disclosure panels, controls, scrubber, gallery overlay, inbox, tools panel, and settings, with extensive embedded design rationale.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a calm, accessible, multi-form-factor visual surface for an interactive wheel-based application while documenting design decisions inline.
